title: La Mia Famiglia
scenario: You meet an Italian exchange student. They ask about your family.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Ciao! Mi chiamo Alex. E tu?
lines.english: Hi! My name is Alex. And you?

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: Mi chiamo Marco. Hai fratelli o sorelle?
lines.english: My name is Marco. Do you have brothers or sisters?

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Sì, ho una sorella e un fratello.
lines.english: Yes, I have a sister and a brother.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Mia sorella ha dodici anni. Mio fratello ha otto anni.
lines.english: My sister is twelve. My brother is eight.

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: E i tuoi genitori? Che lavoro fanno?
lines.english: And your parents? What work do they do?

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Mio padre è ingegnere. Mia madre è insegnante.
lines.english: My father is an engineer. My mother is a teacher.

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: Mia madre è dottoressa. Lavora all'ospedale.
lines.english: My mother is a doctor. She works at the hospital.

lines.speaker: you
lines.english: How nice! Do you have pets?

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: Sì, ho un cane. Si chiama Luna.
lines.english: Yes, I have a dog. Her name is Luna.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Anch'io ho un gatto! Si chiama Micio.
lines.english: I also have a cat! His name is Micio.

lines.speaker: you
lines.italian: Abito in una casa grande con un giardino.
lines.english: I live in a big house with a garden.

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: Io abito in un appartamento in città.
lines.english: I live in an apartment in the city.

lines.speaker: you
lines.english: Do you like living in the city?

lines.speaker: marco
lines.italian: Sì, molto! Ci sono cinema, negozi e ristoranti.
lines.english: Yes, a lot! There are cinemas, shops and restaurants.

practicePrompts.prompt: How would you say you have two brothers?
How would you say your mother is a lawyer?
How would you ask someone where they live?
